articles.title: Lakers Add Pelicans Executive Rohan Ramadas as Assistant GM
articles.original_title: Lakers hire Pelicans exec Rohan Ramadas as assistant GM in latest move to bolster their front office - The Lufkin Daily News
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers have hired Rohan Ramadas, formerly an executive with the New Orleans Pelicans, to serve as an assistant general manager. The move is part of the Lakers' effort to strengthen their front office ahead of the upcoming season.
key_points: Rohan Ramadas joins the Lakers from the Pelicans.
He will assume the role of assistant general manager.
The hire is intended to bolster the Lakers' front‑office operations.

articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers announced the hiring of former New Orleans Pelicans executive Rohan Ramadas as an assistant general manager. Multiple outlets highlighted the appointment, noting his analytical background.
key_points: Rohan Ramadas moves from the Pelicans to the Lakers.

Media sources emphasize his data‑driven approach to roster building.

articles.title: Luka Dončić Reunites With Daughters in Slovenia
articles.original_title: Luka Doncic Reunites With Daughters In Slovenia
articles.summary: After a season‑ending hamstring injury that sidelined him for the Lakers' postseason, Luka Dončić traveled to Slovenia to spend time with his daughters. The visit offers a personal respite and motivation ahead of the upcoming offseason.
key_points: Dončić missed the Lakers' entire playoff run due to a Grade 2 hamstring strain.
He traveled to Slovenia to be with his daughters during the offseason.
The family time is expected to fuel his drive for the next NBA season.

key_points: Dončić received All‑NBA recognition for his scoring and playmaking.

articles.title: Lakers hire Pelicans exec Rohan Ramadas as assistant GM in latest move to bolster their front office
articles.original_title: Lakers hire Pelicans exec Rohan Ramadas as assistant GM in latest move to bolster their front office
articles.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers have hired Rohan Ramadas as their assistant general manager of strategy and data systems, making the first major move of their offseason to bolster their front office under new ownership.  The Lakers announced the decision Tuesday to add Ramadas, who spent the past nine years working with the New Orleans Pelicans, including the past two as their vice president of strategy and basketball operations.

articles.title: Donovan Mitchell Asks ESPN to End Trade Rumors, Wants to Stay in Cleveland
articles.original_title: Donovan Mitchell told Marc Spears of ESPN to “stop the [trade] speculation” and that he wants to return to Cleveland and work on his next deal. Mitchell becomes eligible for a 4YR/$272M contract extension on July 7 or, if he waits until 2027, a 5YR/$350M contract with a no-trade clause.
articles.summary: In a Reddit post, Cleveland Cavaliers star Donovan Mitchell told ESPN reporter Marc Spears to stop speculation about a trade. Mitchell expressed his desire to remain in Cleveland and focus on negotiating a contract extension, with options for a 4‑year/$272 M deal in July or a larger 5‑year/$350 M contract later with a no‑trade clause.
key_points: Mitchell publicly requested an end to trade speculation.
He prefers to stay in Cleveland and work on a new contract.
Eligibility dates: July 7 for a 4‑year/$272 M extension; 2027 for a 5‑year/$350 M deal with a no‑trade clause.
